name: Boomi
what_it_does: Enterprise Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS) for connecting cloud applications, on-premises systems, and data sources. Boomi provides a visual low-code interface for building integration processes, API management, B2B/EDI trading partner management, and master data management. Handles millions of integrations per day for enterprise customers — connecting Salesforce, SAP, NetSuite, databases, FTP servers, and hundreds of other systems. Boomi AtomSphere is the platform; Atoms are the execution engines.
use_cases: Connect enterprise systems (SAP, Salesforce, Oracle) to AI agent data pipelines without writing custom integration code
Expose legacy system data to agents via Boomi API Management — wrap existing APIs with rate limiting, authentication, and transformation
Process B2B EDI transactions automatically with Boomi's EDI trading partner management and route to AI systems
Build master data management pipelines that feed cleaned, deduplicated reference data to agent knowledge bases
Create event-driven integration flows that trigger agent workflows when data changes in source systems
not_for: Small teams and startups — Boomi is enterprise-priced and complex to operate; use n8n, Zapier, or Make for small-scale integration
Developer-centric API-first teams — Boomi's visual builder is excellent for enterprise integration teams but frustrating for code-first developers
Real-time low-latency integration — Boomi excels at batch and near-real-time; use Kafka or event streaming for sub-second latency
best_when: Enterprise organizations needing to integrate dozens of cloud and on-premises business systems with complex data transformations, B2B/EDI, and regulatory compliance requirements.
avoid_when: Your team is technical and code-first, or you have fewer than 5-10 systems to integrate — the operational complexity and cost of Boomi aren't justified.

auth.notes: AtomSphere API uses API tokens (username + API token). Boomi API Management supports OAuth2, API keys, and JWT for managed APIs. SSO/SAML for platform access. Service account tokens for automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier. Trial available with sales engagement.
pricing.paid_tiers: Professional from ~$25K/year; Enterprise from ~$50K/year; custom volume-based pricing
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing with annual contracts. Pricing based on number of connectors, Atoms, and process volume. Dell Technologies acquired Boomi; pricing requires sales engagement.

performance.rate_limits: Process execution limits by plan; Atom-level throughput depends on hardware allocation

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AtomSphere API returns XML/JSON with error codes. Process execution errors appear in Boomi's audit log and can be queried via API. Error messages are somewhat legacy (XML-heavy) in some APIs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Process execution creates new execution instances — not idempotent by default. Process logic can implement idempotency using Boomi's data caching or custom logic. API Management endpoints are idempotent for GET operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Boomi AtomSphere API uses a mix of XML and JSON — some older endpoints are XML-only; newer endpoints support JSON
Atoms (execution engines) can be cloud-hosted (Boomi Cloud Atom) or on-premises — agents must know which Atom type to target for operations
Process deployment requires packaging and deploying to specific Atoms — agents triggering processes must target the correct deployed Atom
Boomi's connector framework requires Boomi licensing per connector type — adding new system connectors may require license changes
AtomSphere API is separate from the managed APIs created via Boomi's API Management — different auth and endpoint patterns
Enterprise pricing and annual contracts mean evaluation requires sales engagement before technical testing is possible

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP authorized. Enterprise-grade security. SAML/SSO. Data residency options globally. Atom-level encryption for sensitive data processing. Dell Technologies ownership adds enterprise compliance credibility.
